insight.summary: Access recovery completed and the reviewer confirmed the console is usable again.
action_items: Confirm the reviewer can sign in and close the ticket.

label_rationale: Confidence is well above the auto-pass line, the tone is non-negative with a concrete action recorded, and the summary makes no absolute claims, so the rubric stays silent.

insight.summary: The note hints at a missing handoff step but the responsible team is still unsure.
action_items: Ask the owner to confirm the intended handoff step.

label_rationale: Confidence falls below the auto-pass line while tone, action, and wording are otherwise in order, so the only rubric flag is the low-confidence one.

insight.summary: The export step failed for the reporting team and no follow-up was recorded on the insight.

label_rationale: A negative-toned insight records no follow-up action, exactly the gap the missing-action flag exists to catch; confidence is high and the wording is plain.

insight.summary: This mapping is guaranteed to be always correct and needs no further checks before rollout.
action_items: Publish the mapping to the shared integration guide.

label_rationale: The summary asserts an absolute, unqualified correctness guarantee, which the rubric treats as unsafe overconfidence; confidence, tone, and action are otherwise clean.

insight.summary: The guide was correct in this checked example and is always reviewed after a peer read before release.
action_items: Merge the guide update after a peer read.

label_rationale: This insight sits exactly on the auto-pass confidence line, and its summary describes a single checked example and a routine peer review without making an absolute, unqualified claim, so it stays clean on both the confidence and the wording checks.

insight.summary: This unclear escalation is certainly always correct and the team recorded no follow-up of any kind.

label_rationale: Low confidence, a negative tone with no recorded action, and an absolute overconfident summary all apply at once, so the rubric raises every flag together in canonical order.
